prompt: Cinematic wide-angle forest photograph, soft diffused daylight. A lone figure stands in a dense forest, surrounded immediately by enormous ancient trees — their trunks wider than a person, bark deeply textured, towering out of frame above. The figure looks small and dwarfed by the giants immediately around them. Yet stretching into the misty background in every direction, the vast majority of trees are visibly thin, young, and small — slender saplings and modest trunks filling the mid-ground and far distance in their hundreds. The contrast is immediate and stark: wherever the figure stands, only giants surround them, yet the forest itself is overwhelmingly populated by small trees. The enormous trees near the figure and the tiny trees of the wider forest are simultaneously visible in the same frame. Mood: quiet wrongness, spatial inevitability, something subtly impossible that feels completely real. Color palette: deep forest greens, warm amber light filtering through canopy, cool blue-grey mist in the distance. Photorealistic, not surreal or cartoonish. Natural documentary photography style. No text, no labels, no numbers, no charts, no annotations, no diagrams, no mathematical notation anywhere in the image.
visual_logic: A figure surrounded by enormous trees wherever they stand, yet the wider forest is overwhelmingly small trees. Arrival always lands near the giants — sampling by presence distorts the true distribution.
